title: Typing Indicator Protocol — Shared Types & Channel Contract
spec:
Define the typing indicator event protocol that will be used across the agent, webui, and desktop packages.

In packages/shared/src/messages.ts, add a TypingIndicatorEvent type:
- company_id: string
- session_id: string  (the expert_session id)
- status: 'typing' | 'idle'

Document (in the same file via a JSDoc comment or adjacent constant) the Supabase Realtime channel naming convention:
- Channel name: `typing:{company_id}:{session_id}`
- Broadcast event name: `typing_status`
- Payload shape: TypingIndicatorEvent

Export the type from packages/shared/src/index.ts (or wherever shared types are re-exported). No runtime logic, no migration — just the shared contract.
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Typing Indicator Protocol

Scenario: TypingIndicatorEvent type is defined and exported
  Given the packages/shared/src/messages.ts file
  When the typing indicator protocol changes are applied
  Then a TypingIndicatorEvent interface/type exists with company_id (string), session_id (string), and status ('typing' | 'idle')
  And the type is exported from the shared package's public surface

Scenario: Channel contract is documented
  Given the shared types file
  When a developer looks for the typing indicator channel name
  Then a constant or JSDoc comment documents the channel pattern as 'typing:{company_id}:{session_id}'
  And the broadcast event name 'typing_status' is documented alongside it

Scenario: No runtime changes outside shared types
  Given the changes in this job
  When the shared package is compiled
  Then only type definitions and documentation are added — no edge functions, no migrations, no UI code

title: Agent — Emit Typing Events When Composing Chat Replies
spec:
In packages/local-agent, emit typing indicator broadcast events via Supabase Realtime when the agent begins and finishes composing a standard chat reply (not for tool-driven work).

Context:
- Expert sessions are tracked in the expert_sessions table and driven by the local-agent daemon
- The local agent already has a Supabase Realtime connection (see packages/local-agent/src/connection.ts)
- Standard chat replies are distinct from tool-driven work (the 'Agent is thinking' path)

Implementation:
1. When the agent begins generating a standard chat reply for an expert session, broadcast a typing_status event with status='typing' on channel `typing:{company_id}:{session_id}`
2. When the reply is fully sent (or on error/timeout), broadcast status='idle' on the same channel
3. Do NOT emit typing events during tool-driven work (tool calls, long-running tasks) — the existing 'Agent is thinking' banner covers those
4. The session_id in the broadcast payload must match the expert_session.id for the current session
5. Use the TypingIndicatorEvent type from packages/shared

Channel: `typing:{company_id}:{session_id}`
Broadcast event name: `typing_status`
Payload: TypingIndicatorEvent (from shared package)
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Agent Emits Typing Events

Scenario: Typing event emitted when composing a chat reply
  Given the local agent is handling an expert session with a known session_id
  When the agent begins generating a standard chat reply
  Then a Supabase Realtime broadcast event 'typing_status' is emitted on channel 'typing:{company_id}:{session_id}'
  And the event payload has status='typing'

Scenario: Idle event emitted when reply is complete
  Given the agent has emitted a typing='typing' event
  When the agent finishes sending the chat reply
  Then a 'typing_status' broadcast event is emitted on the same channel
  And the event payload has status='idle'

Scenario: Idle event emitted on error
  Given the agent has emitted a status='typing' event
  When the reply generation fails with an error
  Then a 'typing_status' event with status='idle' is still emitted
  So that the UI does not get stuck showing a typing indicator

Scenario: No typing events for tool-driven work
  Given the agent is executing tool calls or long-running tasks
  When the agent performs tool-driven work
  Then no typing_status broadcast events are emitted
  And the existing 'Agent is thinking' banner remains the sole indicator for that path

title: Web UI — Typing Indicator Component and Realtime Subscription
spec:
Add a typing indicator to the expert session chat view in packages/webui.

Context:
- The webui uses Supabase Realtime via the useRealtimeTable hook (packages/webui/src/hooks/useRealtimeTable.ts) for postgres_changes
- For broadcast events (not postgres_changes), use supabase.channel().on('broadcast', ...) directly
- The chat view for expert sessions is in the webui — find the relevant component(s)
- The existing 'Agent is thinking' banner must remain unchanged

Implementation:
1. Create a useTypingIndicator(sessionId, companyId) hook that:
   - Subscribes to Supabase Realtime channel 'typing:{companyId}:{sessionId}' for broadcast event 'typing_status'
   - Returns isTyping: boolean
   - Auto-resets isTyping to false after 10 seconds without an 'idle' event (safety timeout)
   - Unsubscribes on unmount
2. Create a TypingIndicator React component that renders an animated typing indicator (e.g., three bouncing dots) when visible
3. Integrate into the expert session chat view: render <TypingIndicator /> when isTyping is true, below the last message
4. Keep the existing 'Agent is thinking' banner unchanged — it handles tool-driven work

Use the TypingIndicatorEvent type and channel constants from packages/shared.
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Web UI Typing Indicator

Scenario: Typing indicator appears when agent starts composing
  Given the user has the expert session chat view open in the webui
  When a 'typing_status' broadcast event with status='typing' is received on the session's channel
  Then an animated typing indicator appears in the chat view below the last message
  And the existing 'Agent is thinking' banner is unaffected

Scenario: Typing indicator disappears when agent finishes
  Given the typing indicator is visible in the chat view
  When a 'typing_status' event with status='idle' is received
  Then the typing indicator is hidden

Scenario: Typing indicator auto-hides after timeout
  Given the typing indicator is visible
  When 10 seconds pass without receiving any 'typing_status' event
  Then the typing indicator automatically hides
  So that a missed 'idle' event does not leave the indicator stuck

Scenario: No duplicate indicators for tool-driven work
  Given the agent is doing tool-driven work
  When the 'Agent is thinking' banner is visible
  Then the typing indicator is NOT also shown
  And the 'Agent is thinking' banner remains the sole indicator for that state

title: Desktop App — Typing Indicator Integration
spec:
Add the typing indicator to the expert session chat view in packages/desktop (Electron renderer).

Context:
- The desktop app is an Electron app with a React renderer in packages/desktop
- It has its own Supabase client and can subscribe to Realtime broadcast channels
- The goal is feature parity with the webui typing indicator

Implementation:
1. Either reuse the useTypingIndicator hook from webui (if there is a shared component package) or recreate it in packages/desktop — subscribe to 'typing:{companyId}:{sessionId}' broadcast channel, return isTyping boolean with 10-second safety timeout
2. Reuse or recreate the TypingIndicator animated component
3. Integrate into the desktop expert session chat view, rendering the indicator when isTyping is true
4. Visual style should match the webui implementation (consistent UX across surfaces)
5. Do not modify the 'Agent is thinking' banner
acceptance_tests:
Feature: Desktop App Typing Indicator

Scenario: Typing indicator appears in desktop chat view
  Given the user has the expert session chat view open in the desktop app
  When a 'typing_status' broadcast event with status='typing' is received
  Then an animated typing indicator appears below the last message

Scenario: Typing indicator disappears on idle event
  Given the typing indicator is visible in the desktop chat view
  When a 'typing_status' event with status='idle' is received
  Then the typing indicator is hidden

Scenario: 10-second safety timeout applies in desktop
  Given the typing indicator is visible in the desktop app
  When 10 seconds pass without any 'typing_status' event
  Then the typing indicator auto-hides

Scenario: Visual consistency with webui
  Given the desktop typing indicator is displayed
  When compared to the webui typing indicator
  Then the animation style and placement are consistent across both surfaces
